Article Title: 'Sabse pehle maine ... ': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's first reaction after getting India cap
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (BCCI Photo)

Tilak Varma's special message

'Dream tha ye'

'Sabse pehle maine apne papa ko bataya'

A record-breaking debut

Poll Vote & Share your view What are your thoughts on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaking Sachin Tendulkar record? It's a historic achievement! It's impressive, but records are meant to be broken. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's dream became a reality on Saturday as the 15-year-old received his India debut cap ahead of the second T20I against England at Old Trafford in Manchester.The left-handed opener created history by becoming India's youngest-ever international cricketer at 15 years and 99 days, breaking Sachin Tendulkar's 36-year-old record.Before the start of the match, India batter Tilak Varma presented Sooryavanshi with his debut cap and congratulated the teenager on the special occasion."Congrats. Pehle cheez hai ki family ke liye aur tere liye bahut proud moment hai. Wo log bahut peeche mehnat kare hain. Bahut proud feel hota hai. Iss cap ko tune earn kiya hai talent aur determination ke saath. Play fearless cricket. Nothing changes here, Play with the right attitude. Make every Indian and country proud. Congratulations. Cap No. 122 - Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. All the best," Tilak said while giving the debut cap to Sooryavanshi.Speaking after receiving his debut cap, an emotional Sooryavanshi said playing for India had always been his biggest dream."Kaafi accha lag raha hai. Dream tha ye. Kisi ke liye India khelna hi sabse badi baat hoti hai. Koi bhi cricket khelna start karta hai international cricket khelne ke aur apne country ko represent karne ke liye, to kaafi proud moment hai," Sooryavanshi said in a video posted by BCCI.The Bihar youngster revealed that his father was the first person he informed after learning that he would make his India debut."Sabse pehle maine apne father ko jaakar room mein bataya. Unko jaakar bola. Wo saath mei stay kar rha hain. Unko sabse pehe bataya. Aur kisi se maine baat nahi ki. Aur jo bhi mere sir hain Romi (Bhinder) sir and mummy. Matlab mere jo saath rehte hain un sab ko bataya. But first maine pehle maine papa ko bataya."Sooryavanshi also shared the message he received from his father before stepping onto the field."Papa khush hue aur bole bas ki jo karte aa rahe abhi tak wahi karna. Pressure nahi hai, bas apne game ko back karna hai."The teenager from Bihar earned his maiden India call-up after a sensational IPL 2026 season and was selected for the T20I squads for the tours of Ireland and England, as well as the Asian Games.By making his debut in Manchester, Sooryavanshi surpassed Sachin Tendulkar's record to become India's youngest international cricketer. Overall, he is the second-youngest player from a Full Member nation to debut in international cricket, behind Pakistan's Hasan Raza.Sooryavanshi made an attacking start, scoring 14 runs off 10 balls with two sixes.He announced himself by hitting Jofra Archer for a six over the wicketkeeper's head before smashing another maximum off Josh Tongue.His innings ended in the fifth over when he charged down the track against Will Jacks but was beaten by the flight of the ball. Jos Buttler completed the stumping to end the teenager's entertaining knock.
Article Summary: Vaibhav sooryavanshi becomes india's youngest-ever international cricketer. The 15-year-old made his debut in the second t20i against england at old trafford. He surpassed sachin tendulkar's record of 36 years and 99 days. The left-handed opener scored 14 runs off 10 balls with two sixes.

Article Title: 'Children are watching': TMC MP objects to 'condom ad' during India-England T20I, seeks BCCI action
Article Text:
Kirti Azad urges BCCI to stop adult ads during cricket matches

Poll Vote & Share your view In your opinion, are there sufficient regulations on advertisements during live sports? Yes, they are adequate. No, they need to be stricter. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress (TMC) MP and former India cricketer Kirti Azad objected to the telecast of a condom advertisement during the India-England T20I match, arguing that such "adult advertisements" should not be aired during live cricket broadcasts watched by millions of families and children.Calling on the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) to take note of the issue, Azad said the timing of the advertisement was inappropriate.In a post on X, Azad wrote, "Children are watching live the international T20 match between India versus England being played at Old Trafford. Isn't it a shame that an adult advertisement of Durex comes on the screen. BCCI to take note."Speaking to reporters later, Azad said the issue was not about a particular brand but about the suitability of such advertisements during live cricket broadcasts watched by families."This is an adult advertisement. Children watch the match with their parents. They are children, under 16 years old. In this country, cricket is considered like a religion. When I was watching it, 44 crore people were watching it, and an adult advertisement appeared during the match," he said.He said such advertisements put parents in an uncomfortable position while raising questions among children."Parents don't know where to look, and children don't understand it.They ask what it is. What effect will it have on their minds? This is a very big question, and the BCCI should answer it. How are they allowing all this?" Azad said.The TMC MP also questioned why adult advertisements were being aired during live cricket when advertisements related to alcohol and cigarettes are prohibited."Alcohol and cigarette advertisements are all banned, and they should be. So why adult advertisements? And that too between overs. I raise a big question over its validity," he said.Azad said the BCCI should examine the issue and take appropriate action."The BCCI has to look into it. If needed, then yes, I will raise it in Parliament. I will definitely raise it," he added.The India tour of England is being held from July 1 to July 19, featuring a five-match T20I series followed by a three-match ODI series across various venues in England and Wales. England currently lead the T20I series 1-0 after their victory at Old Trafford.
Article Summary: Kirti azad objects to telecast of condom advertisement during india-england t20i match. Trinamool congress (tmc) mp says such "adult advertisements" should not be aired during live cricket broadcasts. Azad calls on the board of control for cricket in india (bcci) to take note of the issue.

Article Title: 'It is now over': Tearful Neymar announces instant international retirement after Brazil’s shock World Cup defeat to Norway
Article Text:
Neymar has announced instant international retirement after Brazil’s shock 2-1 World Cup defeat to Norway in the Round of 16/ Image: X

Neymar confirms international retirement after heartbreaking World Cup exit

Brazil's Neymar (10) celebrates after scoring from a penalty kick during the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Brazil and Norway in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 5, 2026. (AP Photo/Adam Hunger)

Brazil's Neymar (10) reacts at the end of the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Brazil and Norway in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 5, 2026. (AP Photo/Adam Hunger)

"I tried. I tried. It started here at Met Life Stadium, and I finished here. It is now over."

A record-breaking Brazil career comes to a close

Brazil's Marquinhos, left, Neymar (10) and Raphinha react after losing to Norway the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Brazil and Norway in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 5, 2026. (AP Photo/Seth Wenig)

Club future remains undecided as Ancelotti looks ahead





"I don't know what will happen from now on, I don't know about next year.

"It may be that when December comes, I'll want to retire. I'm living year to year now. We will see what my heart decides. It depends on what my heart says later in the year."

Brazil's Neymar (10) walks off the field with teammates after the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Brazil and Norway in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 5, 2026. (AP Photo/Seth Wenig)

"We must continue to work and improve, and find new ideas,"

"I believe that this loss is not the end but the beginning of a new cycle."

Brazil's FIFA World Cup campaign ended in heartbreak on Sunday night, and with it came the end of one of the greatest international careers in the nation's history. Moments after Brazil suffered a 2-1 Round of 16 defeat to Norway at MetLife Stadium, an emotional Neymar confirmed that he had played his final match for the Seleção, bringing a remarkable 16-year journey in the famous yellow shirt to a close. The 34-year-old left the pitch in tears after scoring a stoppage-time penalty that proved to be his final goal for Brazil, before announcing his retirement from international football as the country's all-time leading goalscorer.Brazil entered the knockout tie hoping to extend their pursuit of a sixth World Cup title, but Norway produced one of the biggest upsets of the tournament with a 2-1 victory to eliminate Carlo Ancelotti's side in the Round of 16. Erling Haaland's late double turned the match in Norway's favour before Neymar converted a penalty deep into stoppage time, although it proved to be nothing more than a consolation.When the final whistle sounded, the Brazilian forward immediately sat down on the pitch and broke down in tears as teammates gathered around him in an attempt to console the veteran attacker. The emotions reflected more than Brazil's elimination, as Neymar already knew the announcement he was about to make.Speaking after the match, Neymar confirmed that his international career had come to an end.The announcement brought the curtain down on a Brazil career that began with his senior debut in 2010 and spanned four FIFA World Cups, two Olympic Games and more than a decade as the face of Brazilian football.Neymar retires as Brazil's all-time leading men's goalscorer after scoring 80 goals while also providing 59 assists across 130 international appearances over a 16-year career. He also finishes as Brazil's second-highest appearance maker, with only one player having represented the Seleção more times.His international résumé stretches far beyond goals alone. Neymar represented Brazil at four FIFA World Cups and featured in two Olympic Games, winning a silver medal in 2012 before captaining his country to its long-awaited Olympic gold medal on home soil in Rio de Janeiro in 2016.His inclusion in Brazil's squad for the 2026 World Cup had been celebrated across the country following a lengthy battle with injuries, but his tournament proved frustrating. Neymar made only two substitute appearances, playing a combined 37 minutes across Brazil's five matches. His only goal came from the penalty spot in stoppage time against Norway, ultimately becoming the final goal of his international career.Although Neymar has now confirmed the end of his time with Brazil, he stopped short of announcing his retirement from football altogether.Earlier this year, speaking about his future, the forward admitted he was still undecided over how much longer he would continue playing professionally.While Neymar's international chapter has closed, Brazil manager Carlo Ancelotti insisted the defeat should represent the beginning of a rebuilding process rather than its conclusion.Appointed as Brazil head coach in May, Ancelotti ruled out stepping away despite overseeing Brazil's earliest World Cup exit since 1990, with the Seleção failing to reach the quarter-finals for the first time in 36 years.Ancelotti said in his post-match press conference.Norway's victory sends them into the World Cup quarter-finals, where they will face the winner of the Round of 16 meeting between Mexico and England. For Brazil, however, the tournament will forever be remembered as the stage on which one of the country's greatest footballers brought an extraordinary international career to its emotional conclusion.
Article Summary: Neymar retires as brazil's all-time leading men's goalscorer after scoring 80 goals while also providing 59 assists over a 16-year career. The 34-year-old left the pitch in tears after scoring a stoppage-time penalty that proved to be his final goal for brazil. Brazil suffered a 2-1 round of 16 defeat to norway at metlife stadium.

Article Title: 'I will finish when I decide': Cristiano Ronaldo shuts down retirement speculation
Article Text:
Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo speaks to reporters during a press conference ahead of his team's World Cup round of 16 soccer match against Spain, Sunday, July 5, 2026, in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas. (AP Photo/Julio Cortez)

Ronaldo confirms his final World Cup

"God has been generous to me"

Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo speaks to reporters during a press conference ahead of his team's World Cup round of 16 soccer match against Spain, Sunday, July 5, 2026, in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas. (AP Photo/Julio Cortez)

A tournament already filled with historic milestones

Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo (7) celebrates scoring a goal on a penalty kick during the World Cup round of 32 soccer match between Portugal and Croatia in Toronto, Thursday, July 2, 2026. (Sammy Kogan/The Canadian Press via AP)

Portugal face Spain with quarter-final place at stake

Croatia's Luka Modric (10) hugs Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo (7) after the World Cup round of 32 soccer match between Portugal and Croatia in Toronto, Thursday, July 2, 2026. (Sammy Kogan/The Canadian Press via AP)

Cristiano Ronaldo has confirmed that the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be the final tournament of his extraordinary international career, with Portugal's captain revealing he will leave the global stage "with a clear conscience" regardless of how his nation's campaign ends.The announcement came during a press conference ahead of Portugal's Round of 16 meeting with Spain in Dallas, where the 41-year-old addressed growing speculation about his future after another record-breaking World Cup. While making it clear that his immediate focus remains on helping Portugal progress, Ronaldo acknowledged that the tournament marks the closing chapter of a career that has redefined longevity in international football.Speaking to reporters on the eve of Portugal's knockout clash, Ronaldo admitted the moment he had long avoided discussing had finally arrived."I want to enjoy it as much as possible, because it will be my last World Cup," Ronaldo said. "But I hope that tomorrow won't be my last game in the World Cup."Later in the press conference, he expanded on his thoughts while insisting that the priority remained Portugal's performance rather than his own farewell."You always ask the same question, whether this is the last one. We'll see. I don't want to draw attention to that, it isn't important. What's important is whether we go through," he said.The Portugal captain nevertheless acknowledged that, after more than two decades representing his country, he was completely at peace with everything he had achieved."The day will come, but, honestly, whatever happens tomorrow, I will leave with a clear conscience, not 100% but 1,000%, because I have given everything to football. I don't need it; I live well, but it's about passion. I play football because I love it."Ronaldo also reflected on a career that has taken him from making his Portugal debut against Kazakhstan in August 2003 to becoming the most prolific goalscorer in international football history.Now with 146 goals in 232 appearances for Portugal, he said lifting the World Cup would not define how he views his legacy."I'm not missing anything; God has been generous to me," Ronaldo said. "I won't be more Cristiano or less Cristiano if I win the World Cup or not."The veteran forward explained that his motivation has never been driven by necessity or personal recognition."Regardless of what happens tomorrow I'm not going to exert pressure on myself that I must win. You have to enjoy every match at a huge competition like the World Cup. I think I'm not doing so bad. I've scored three goals, others have done better but I think I'm doing not so bad."He added that criticism has become part of his career and has ultimately strengthened him."They've tried to kill me for 23 years. There's no point paying too much attention to it. It's part of it. People in Portugal have faith, they never fail us, they're always on our side, my side. All the rest is rubbish. It doesn't matter at all. I'm not lacking anything in life. God has given me everything, more than I ever expected. Age gives you maturity and experience. I'm thankful even for the attacks I receive. That's how you grow as a person; it has made me stronger. "With a smile, Ronaldo concluded: "You have to enjoy every day, like the last World Cup, which it will be, but hopefully, hopefully, tomorrow is not the last day. Hopefully. And then you can kill me a bit more."Despite confirming this will be his final World Cup, Ronaldo stressed that the decision on when to end his international career rests entirely with him."Whether I am playing or not, I will always have an important role with this national team. I will finish when I decide to finish, as I said years ago. To be honest, whatever happens tomorrow, I will leave with a clear conscience, not 100%, but 1000%. Because I've given everything in football," he said.Ronaldo arrives at the Spain match having already added several more records to an unparalleled international career.Earlier in the tournament, he became the oldest player ever to score in a men's World Cup knockout match, surpassing former Portugal teammate Pepe, who had previously held the record after scoring at 39 years and nine months.He also extended another remarkable achievement by becoming the first player to score in six different FIFA World Cups, having now found the net in the 2010, 2014, 2018, 2022 and 2026 editions, in addition to his earlier tournament appearances.His three goals at the current World Cup have once again demonstrated that, even at 41, he remains one of Portugal's decisive attacking figures.Portugal's Round of 16 encounter with Spain now carries even greater significance after Ronaldo's announcement.Defeat in Dallas would bring the five-time Ballon d'Or winner's World Cup career to an immediate conclusion, while victory would extend his farewell for at least one more match and keep alive his pursuit of the only major international trophy still missing from an otherwise glittering career.Speculation surrounding Ronaldo's retirement had intensified before the tournament after his sister, Katia Aveiro, suggested this would be his "last dance" with Portugal. "Enjoy it while it lasts," Aveiro said. "It's ending soon. The info I have, from a reliable source… this is his last dance. I believe it’s not today that they’ll say goodbye, but it’s coming soon. I strongly believe this is the farewell." She added: "So enjoy it a lot because it will be difficult to find someone after 200 goals. I’m talking about the Portuguese national team." Now, the captain himself has confirmed that the 2026 World Cup will indeed be his final appearance on football's biggest stage.For now, however, Ronaldo's focus remains firmly on the present. Portugal stand one victory away from the quarter-finals, and the man who has spent 23 years rewriting football history is determined to ensure his final World Cup story does not end just yet.
Article Summary: Cristiano ronaldo has confirmed that the 2026 fifa world cup will be the final tournament of his extraordinary international career. Portugal's captain reveals he will leave the global stage "with a clear conscience" regardless of how his nation's campaign ends.

Article Title: Jude Bellingham joins elite group of only two players in World Cup history to score World Cup brace in under 100 seconds
Article Text:
England's Jude Bellingham (10) celebrates after scoring his team's first goal during the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Mexico and England in Mexico City, Sunday, July 5, 2026. (AP Photo/Natacha Pisarenko)

Bellingham joins Kroos and Mbappé in World Cup history

Historic night at the Estadio Azteca

England's Jude Bellingham (10) celebrates with teammates after scoring his second goal during the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Mexico and England in Mexico City, Sunday, July 5, 2026. (AP Photo/Natacha Pisarenko)





Brace proves decisive as England progress

Jude Bellingham etched his name into the FIFA World Cup record books after scoring the third-fastest brace in tournament history during England's dramatic 3-2 Round of 16 victory over co-hosts Mexico at the Estadio Azteca. The England midfielder struck twice in the space of just 98 seconds, joining an exclusive list headed only by Germany's Toni Kroos and France's Kylian Mbappé. His quickfire double not only transformed the knockout contest but also helped England reach the quarter-finals after surviving a late Mexican fightback.The 23-year-old produced his decisive burst midway through the first half as England capitalised on a brief spell of dominance inside one of world football's most intimidating stadiums.Bellingham opened the scoring in the 36th minute when Bukayo Saka broke down the right flank and delivered a precise cross into the penalty area. Timing his run perfectly, the midfielder rose between the defenders to head home from close range and hand England a deserved lead.Mexico had scarcely restarted before England struck again.Just 98 seconds after his opener, Bellingham completed his brace. England won possession high up the pitch before launching another swift attack through Saka, whose second dangerous delivery once again found Bellingham arriving inside the six-yard box.The Real Madrid star calmly converted from close range to double England's advantage and stun the home crowd at the Azteca.The performance also carried major historical significance, as Jude Bellingham became England’s joint-third highest goalscorer in FIFA World Cup history with his two strikes against Mexico. He now has five World Cup goals to his name, moving level with Sir Geoff Hurst’s long-standing tournament tally.The two goals were separated by only one minute and 38 seconds, making Bellingham's brace the third-fastest ever recorded at a FIFA World Cup.Only two players have scored a quicker World Cup double. Toni Kroos tops the list after netting twice in one minute and 10 seconds during Germany's historic 7-1 victory over Brazil in the 2014 semi-final, while Kylian Mbappé occupies second place after scoring twice in one minute and 35 seconds during France's Round of 16 victory over Argentina at the 2022 World Cup.His two goals also made him the first player to score a World Cup brace at the Estadio Azteca since Diego Maradona achieved the feat for Argentina against Belgium in the 1986 semi-final. Crucially, the stadium had not hosted another FIFA World Cup tournament in the four decades that followed, meaning no World Cup goals, let alone a brace, had been scored there since Maradona’s performance until Bellingham broke that sequence.His opener was equally significant for the hosts, becoming the first goal Mexico had conceded during the 2026 World Cup after keeping four consecutive clean sheets through the group stage and Round of 32.Although England established a commanding two-goal lead, Mexico ensured the contest remained alive throughout.Julián Quiñones pulled one back before half-time, Harry Kane restored England's two-goal cushion from the penalty spot after the interval, and Raúl Jiménez converted a penalty of his own following a VAR review to reduce the deficit to 3-2.England then played the closing stages with 10 men after Jarell Quansah was sent off following a VAR review, forcing Thomas Tuchel's side to withstand relentless late pressure before securing their place in the quarter-finals.While Kane added another important goal and Jordan Pickford produced key saves during the closing minutes, the defining spell of the evening came during Bellingham's remarkable 98-second burst. It was a sequence that not only changed the match but also secured the England midfielder a permanent place among the fastest goalscorers in FIFA World Cup history. Next up, England move on to the quarter-finals in Miami, where they will face Norway at Hard Rock Stadium.
Article Summary: Jude bellingham scored twice in the space of just 98 seconds during england's 3-2 round of 16 victory over co-hosts mexico at the estadio azteca. The 23-year-old's brace is the third-fastest ever recorded at a fifa world cup. Toni kroos tops the list after netting twice in one minute and 10 seconds during germany's historic 7-1 victory over brazil in the 2014 semi-final.

Article Title: Norway's future queen Princess Ingrid Alexandra hugs Erling Haaland after win over Brazil in a viral video
Article Text:
Erling Haaland. Image via: Getty Images

Erling Haaland's embrace with Norway's future queen draws attention

Norway's future queen and her brother at the World Cup. Image via: Getty Images

Check out the video here:

Fans react to Erling Haaland and Norway's future queen Princess Ingrid Alexandra’s moment

Erling Haaland shines in Norway's victory over Brazil

Haaland against Gabriel Magalhães of Brazil. Image via: Buda Mendes/ Getty Images

Princess Ingrid Alexandra shared an embrace with Erling Haaland in the moments after Norway eliminated Brazil from the World Cup, a scene that spread quickly across social media Sunday night. Norway's future queen and her brother, Prince Sverre Magnus, have followed the team throughout the tournament, and their presence at MetLife Stadium capped one of the biggest results in Norwegian football history.The 22-year-old princess and her 20-year-old brother wore Norway scarves over formal attire as they watched Haaland's brace send Brazil home in the round of 16.The siblings have been fixtures at Norway's matches since the group stage, including the win over Senegal on June 22. Ingrid Alexandra's appearance at that game was actually a late substitution of her own. Her father, Crown Prince Haakon, had originally planned to attend with Sverre Magnus before stepping aside following a family health matter, and Ingrid joined her brother instead.Their visibility has been significant. Their mother, Crown Princess Mette-Marit, underwent a successful lung transplant in Oslo in mid-June, and the family has managed a difficult stretch of personal news since.The World Cup run has given the siblings a very public and much lighter storyline to attach themselves to.Reaction across social media leaned heavily into the novelty of royalty mixing with one of the world's biggest football stars."Royalty meets royalty. Princess Ingrid Alexandra = class act," one fan wrote, tagging the moment with Norway's flag.Another summed up the broader arc of Haaland's tournament: "Haaland just casually meeting royalty now, insane."A third fan focused on the emotion of the scene itself: "Happiness is clearly visible on their faces."And one more framed it as vindication for Norway's improbable run: "Norway national team royal validation."None of the royal attention would carry the same weight without what happened on the field. Haaland scored twice in the final 12 minutes, heading in a Schjelderup cross in the 79th minute before doubling the lead with a left-footed strike from outside the box in the 90th. Goalkeeper Orjan Nyland saved a first-half penalty from Bruno Guimaraes, and Neymar's stoppage-time penalty proved only a consolation as Brazil suffered its earliest World Cup exit since 1990.The win sends Norway into its first-ever World Cup quarterfinal, where it will face the winner of Mexico versus England. Haaland's brace also pulled him level with Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe atop the Golden Boot standings, giving Norway's biggest star and its biggest fans in the royal box plenty more to watch for on July 11.
Article Summary: Princess ingrid alexandra and erling haaland shared an embrace after norway's win over brazil. The siblings have been fixtures at norway's matches since the group stage. Their mother, crown princess mette-marit, underwent a successful lung transplant in june.

Article Title: 'I went up to Lionel Messi after the game. He said ... ': Vozinha reveals Argentina skipper's words after FIFA World Cup exit
Article Text:
Tired of too many ads? go ad free now

Tired of too many ads? go ad free now

Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha has revealed the heartfelt message he received from Lionel Messi after his side's courageous performance against Argentina at the FIFA World Cup.Despite losing 3-2 after extra time, World Cup debutants Cape Verde earned widespread praise for pushing defending champions Argentina to the limit. Goalkeeper Vozinha played a crucial role in keeping his team in the contest, making eight saves against an Argentine attack led by Messi.After the final whistle, Messi and Vozinha shared an emotional moment, with the Argentina captain acknowledging the remarkable effort of the Cape Verde side.“I went up to Messi after the game. He hugged me and said, 'You are great. Your people should be proud of you.' That was incredible for me,” said Vozinha.The veteran goalkeeper described Messi's gesture as one of the most special moments of his career. After thanking the Argentine star, Vozinha also asked if they could exchange jerseys.“Hearing words like that from someone like Leo Messi means a lot to me. I thanked him and said, 'Thank you, Leo. You are the best.' Then I asked if we could swap shirts. Leo said he would give it to me in the tunnel after the interviews," he added.“Moments like these remain engraved in your heart forever," he said.While Cape Verde's historic World Cup run came to an end, the team's fearless display won admiration around the football world. Messi once again led Argentina from the front, scoring his 20th World Cup goal — extending his own record and moving two goals clear of Kylian Mbappé on the tournament's all-time scoring list.Argentina eventually advanced to the Round of 16, but only after surviving one of the toughest tests of their title defence against an inspired Cape Verde.
Article Summary: Cape verde goalkeeper vozinha has revealed the heartfelt message he received from lionel messi after his side's courageous performance against argentina at the fifa world cup. Cape verde lost 3-2 after extra time, but earned widespread praise for their performance.

Article Title: Dianna Russini's unexpected reaction to Brittany Mahomes' roast over Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding sparks buzz
Article Text:
Dianna Russini's unexpected reaction to Brittany Mahomes' roast over Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding sparks buzz(Getty)

Brittany Mahomes wedding photo joke unexpectedly draws Dianna Russini into the conversation

Dianna Russini liked Brittany Mahomes roast video(Instagram)

Mike Vrabel 's appearance with wife Jen added another talking point

The Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding created plenty of memorable moments. Yet one conversation started after the ceremony ended. NFL insider Dianna Russini became part of the online buzz when fans noticed her reaction to a viral comedy video about Brittany Mahomes . The clip had nothing to do with football, but it quickly spread across social media because it centered on one question many fans had already been asking after the wedding.The joke played on Brittany Mahomes' eagerness to share photos from the highly private celebration. Russini's interaction with the post soon caught fans' attention and sparked another round of discussion around one of the biggest celebrity events of the year.The viral clip came from writer and comedian Taylor Wolfe. The skit imagined Brittany Mahomes repeatedly asking Patrick Mahomes when she could finally post pictures from the Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding.The joke worked because the wedding remained unusually secretive. Guests reportedly signed non-disclosure agreements, phones were heavily restricted and very few photos surfaced while the celebrations took place inside Madison Square Garden in New York City on July 3.In the comedy sketch, Brittany Mahomes keeps pressing Patrick Mahomes for permission to upload pictures, only to receive the same answer each time.Wolfe also throws in celebrity names including Hugh Grant, Miranda Lambert and Madonna, making fun of the excitement surrounding the star-studded guest list rather than targeting any real events from the ceremony.The video quickly picked up steam on Instagram, collecting more than 60,000 reactions. Soon after, social media users noticed that Dianna Russini had liked the post. Screenshots spread online, and many fans began discussing her unexpected reaction instead of the skit itself.Patrick and Brittany Mahomes were among the biggest NFL names at the wedding. After the restrictions eased, both shared photos from the celebration. Patrick posted pictures from the event, while Brittany uploaded several images showing the couple in coordinated formal outfits, giving fans one of the first official looks inside the wedding weekend.Russini's name has remained in the spotlight for months because of controversy involving New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel. Earlier this year, photos of the two together at a luxury hotel in Arizona fueled widespread speculation online. Both maintained that the meeting reflected a reporter-source relationship, while no evidence has emerged to support claims beyond those publicly denied.Russini later resigned from The Athletic. In her resignation letter to executive editor Steven Ginsberg, she wrote, "The Athletic supported me unequivocally, expressed confidence in my work and pride in my journalism" when the controversy first surfaced. She also added, "In the days that followed, unfortunately, commentators in various media have engaged in self-feeding speculation that is simply unmoored from the facts. "Vrabel also addressed his family while speaking during a New England Patriots press conference on May 27. "I appreciate, my family is great. I love Jen, I love the boys... this spring is focused on really the coaching staff, the players."Just days later, Vrabel and his wife, Jen, made a rare public appearance together at the Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding. Their arrival attracted attention on its own. At the same time, Russini's reaction to the viral Brittany Mahomes roast kept her name in the headlines, turning an online comedy sketch into one of the weekend's most talked-about NFL side stories.
Article Summary: Nfl insider dianna russini liked a viral comedy video about brittany mahomes. The joke played on mahomes' eagerness to share photos from the taylor swift and travis kelce wedding. The video quickly picked up steam on instagram, collecting more than 60,000 reactions.

Article Title: After Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut, BCCI vice-president sends strong message to critics
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (BCCI Photo)

Youngest India international



England take series lead



NEW DELHI: Board of Control for Cricket in India ( BCCI ) vice-president Rajiv Shukla has defended the selection process after teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi made his India debut, saying the board gives opportunities purely on merit.Shukla dismissed social media claims that Sooryavanshi was being unfairly overlooked and said he had always believed the youngster would get his chance at the right time."Huge congratulations to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on his debut and for receiving this opportunity. The BCCI does not treat anyone unfairly; those who deserve it get their chance. There was a lot of uproar on social media claiming that opportunities weren't being given; people were criticising both the coach and the captain. But that wasn't the case," Shukla told ANI.He added that he was confident Sooryavanshi would make the most of the opportunity."I had said just the other day that when the right moment arrived, he would certainly get his chance, and yesterday, he did. I believe he will perform well in the future and truly come into his own. It is an excellent decision by the team management, and we should appreciate it," he said.Sooryavanshi created history after being named in India's playing XI for the second T20I against England in Manchester.At 15 years and 99 days, the Bihar batter became the youngest player to represent India in international cricket across all formats. He also became the youngest player from a Test-playing nation to make his T20I debut.The left-handed opener made an entertaining start to his international career, scoring 14 off 10 balls with two sixes before being stumped by Will Jacks.In the process, he surpassed the previous records held by Sachin Tendulkar and Piyush Chawla to become India's youngest international cricketer.India posted 190/7 after useful contributions from Abhishek Sharma (43), Shreyas Iyer (37), Ishan Kishan (49) and Tilak Varma, who remained unbeaten on 24 off 11 balls. Sam Curran starred with the ball for England, returning figures of 3/33.England's chase got off to a nightmare start as Phil Salt and Jos Buttler were dismissed for ducks in the opening over - the first time both England openers had fallen for ducks in a T20I innings.Captain Harry Brook then changed the momentum with a blistering 39 off just 15 balls before Jacob Bethell anchored the chase with an unbeaten 76 off 46 deliveries. Bethell shared a crucial partnership with Tom Banton (39) before finishing the game alongside Jofra Archer.England reached the target in 19 overs to win by four wickets and take a 1-0 lead in the five-match series after the opening T20I was washed out.
Article Summary: Bcci vice-president rajiv shukla defends selection process. Teen sensation vaibhav sooryavanshi made his india debut against england. The bihar batter became the youngest player to represent india in all formats. England reached the target in 19 overs to win by four wickets and take a 1-0 lead.

Article Title: 'Thank you': Zlatan Ibrahimović and Thierry Henry pay emotional tributes after Neymar’s international retirement
Article Text:
Zlatan Ibrahimović and Thierry Henry paid tribute to Neymar after his international retirement, calling him a generational talent/ Image: X

Henry: 'Thank you for what you've done'

Brazil's Neymar (10) walks off the field with teammates after the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Brazil and Norway in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 5, 2026. (AP Photo/Seth Wenig)

World Cup Today

Ibrahimović reflects on Neymar's legacy

An international career comes to a close

Zlatan Ibrahimović and Thierry Henry, currently working together as star pundits and analysts for FOX Sports during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, paid tribute to Neymar following his decision to retire from international football after Brazil's Round of 16 exit. The 34-year-old confirmed that Brazil's 2-1 defeat to Norway would be his final appearance for the Seleção, ending a 16-year international career that saw him become the country's all-time leading goalscorer with 80 goals in 129 appearances. While Brazil's campaign ended in disappointment, both analysts focused on Neymar's lasting influence on the game rather than the circumstances of his farewell.Neymar announced his retirement shortly after Brazil were eliminated, having made two substitute appearances at the tournament. He came off the bench against Scotland in the group stage before scoring from the penalty spot in stoppage time against Norway, his only goal of the 2026 World Cup.Speaking on FOX Sports', Thierry Henry emphasized Neymar's broader impact on football."Thank you. This is a guy I would've paid to watch. Simple as that," Henry said.The former France striker added: "We can talk about tactics. We can talk about whatever it is ...but this guy could play anywhere, at any time, in any style. He could've made anyone, and he did make anyone, love the game with the way he was, his goals, his smile, the way he went about it and his skills."Anyone young wanted to be Neymar. You wanted to be Neymar. This guy changed the game with the way he was playing it. So, I don't want to talk about a World Cup and the way he finished. I want to talk about what he has given to the game. Neymar was, and always will be, an outstanding player."Thank you for what you've done, I've enjoyed it."Zlatan Ibrahimović also praised Neymar, highlighting both his talent and the expectations that shaped his career."He will be remembered as the great footballer that came over to Europe, and before he came over to Europe, he was doing his thing in Brazil," Ibrahimović said."When he was at Barcelona, he did good. When he was at PSG, he did good. But I think people will say also that he could've done more than he did because people wanted him to win the Ballon d'Or and he didn't win."But an amazing talent, an amazing player and the skill set he had was just crazy. So, sad moment for Brazilian football."Neymar debuted for Brazil in 2010 and represented his country at four FIFA World Cups. He also competed in two Olympic Games, winning silver in 2012 before captaining Brazil to gold on home soil in 2016.He retires as Brazil's all-time leading scorer with 80 goals in 129 appearances, surpassing Pelé's long-standing record. Injuries limited his involvement at the 2026 World Cup to just 37 minutes across two substitute appearances, with his stoppage-time penalty against Norway marking the final goal of his international career.Brazil's elimination closed the chapter on one of the defining international careers of the modern era. Norway advanced to the quarter-finals, where they will face England following the Three Lions' 3-2 victory over Mexico.
Article Summary: Zlatan ibrahimović and thierry henry paid tribute to neymar after his international retirement. The 34-year-old confirmed that brazil's 2-1 defeat to norway would be his final appearance for the seleção. Both analysts focused on neymar's lasting influence on the game rather than the circumstances of his farewell.

Article Title: 'Remember the name Cape Verde': Ashwin lauds FIFA World Cup debutants
Article Text:
Messi shoots the ball during the match between Argentina and Cape Verde (Agency Image)

Ashwin post

Former India off-spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has showered praise on FIFA World Cup debutants Cabo Verde after their remarkable campaign came to an end with a narrow 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina in the Round of 32.Although the African nation bowed out of the tournament, their performances throughout the competition earned admiration from fans and experts alike. Cabo Verde announced themselves on the world stage by holding European champions Spain to a goalless draw before securing draws against Saudi Arabia and former world champions Uruguay to reach the knockout phase in their maiden FIFA World Cup appearance.Their dream run almost continued against Lionel Messi 's Argentina. Cabo Verde twice fought back to level the scores and forced the reigning champions into extra time before an unfortunate own goal from Diney Borges in the 111th minute proved to be the difference.Impressed by the team's fearless performances, Ashwin took to X to applaud Cabo Verde and urged fans to remember the name of the tournament debutants."Whatever happens from here on in. Remember the name "CAPE VERDE" What a team and what a show they have put on so far this World Cup. #FIFAworldcup," posted the Indian cricket legend.Cabo Verde made a confident start against Argentina and threatened early through Ryan Mendes before Messi produced a moment of brilliance to break the deadlock in the 29th minute.The Argentine captain controlled a diagonal pass from Lisandro Martinez before blasting his finish into the roof of the net to register his seventh goal of the tournament.The underdogs responded strongly after the interval. Deroy Duarte finished clinically from a tight angle after being played through by Mendes, becoming the first Cabo Verde player to score in a FIFA World Cup knockout match.Argentina regained the lead at the start of extra time when Lisandro Martinez scored from close range following a corner, but Cabo Verde refused to surrender. Sidny Lopes Cabral curled home a superb equaliser in the 103rd minute to make it 2-2 and keep their hopes alive.The decisive blow came in the 111th minute when Messi's corner found Cristian Romero, whose header deflected off Diney Borges and into the net for the winning goal. Cabo Verde came close to forcing a penalty shootout in the closing stages, only for Emiliano Martinez to produce a crucial save from Lopes Cabral's powerful free-kick.Argentina have now advanced to the Round of 16, where they will take on Egypt, while Cabo Verde leave the tournament with their reputation greatly enhanced after one of the most memorable debut campaigns in FIFA World Cup history.
Article Summary: Cabo verde lost 3-2 to argentina in the round of 32 of the fifa world cup. Former india off-spinner ravichandran ashwin has showered praise on the african nation. Ashwin urged fans to remember the name of the tournament debutants. Cabo verde announced themselves on the world stage by holding spain to a goalless draw.

Article Title: Why adding a $130 million star to Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves may be the Lakers' biggest mistake?
Article Text:
Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. Image via: Joe Murphy/ NBAE via Getty Images

The Walker Kessler trade has reshaped the Los Angeles Lakers' frontcourt, and it cost them nearly everything left in the vault. Utah sent the 7-foot-2 center to Los Angeles this week in a sign-and-trade that pairs him with Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves, but the price included four premium draft assets.Whether that gamble pays off now hinges on health, fit, and a contract some analysts already consider an overpay before Kessler plays a single game in purple and gold.Kessler's new deal runs four years at $130 million, an annual average of $32.5 million, with a player option in the final season and a full trade kicker attached. Bleacher Report 's Zach Buckley flagged the deal as one of the offseason's steepest overpays, noting Kessler has never made an All-Star or All-NBA roster and that his contract "feels rich for Walker Kessler." Buckley pointed to Kessler's limited scoring range and his 127 total appearances over the past three seasons, including just five games last year after shoulder surgery.The bigger concern isn't the salary alone. It's what Los Angeles surrendered to get him. Four first-round assets, split between outright picks and swaps, represent nearly all the draft capital the Lakers had left to trade.The move traces directly back to Doncic. According to Dan Woike of The Athletic, Kessler was "a player Dončić wanted at center most of all," and a team source believed he'd fit "perfectly" alongside Doncic and Reaves.With LeBron James departing the roster this offseason, the Lakers moved quickly to give their franchise cornerstone the rim-running, shot-blocking presence he'd been requesting.Kessler brings genuine two-way upside when healthy, a 68.1% career shooter who thrives on lobs, cuts, and putbacks, exactly the kind of finishing Doncic's pick-and-roll game demands. The question is durability. He's played through recurring injury issues, and the Lakers now have no realistic path to recoup draft capital if this doesn't work.The Lakers enter training camp with their highest-stakes bet of the offseason already placed. How Kessler performs in his first extended run in a major market, and how quickly he returns from shoulder surgery, will determine whether this trade defines a new contending core or becomes the move Lakers fans point to when explaining what went wrong.
Article Summary: Walker kessler's new deal runs four years at $130 million, an annual average of $32.5 million. Bleacher report's zach buckley flagged the deal as one of the offseason's steepest overpays. Four first-round assets represent nearly all the draft capital the lakers had left to trade.

Article Text:
NFL Trade Rumors: Kansas City Chiefs and Baltimore Ravens emerge as top landing spots for $12.3 million Panthers WR(Getty)

USA Today's Anthony Rizzuti listed the Chiefs and Ravens among potential landing spots

Day 3 draft pick





What are insiders saying about the NFL trade rumors?

Contract details and salary cap implications

How a potential trade could impact both teams

Xavier Legette's future with the Carolina Panthers has become one of the more intriguing storylines ahead of training camp. After two underwhelming seasons, the former first-round pick has slipped down Carolina's wide receiver depth chart following the arrivals of Tetairoa McMillan and rookie Chris Brazzell II. Those developments have fueled fresh NFL trade rumors, with USA Today's Anthony Rizzuti naming the Kansas City Chiefs and Baltimore Ravens among the most logical destinations if the Panthers decide to move on from the 25-year-old wide receiver before Week 1.The Kansas City Chiefs stand out as one of the strongest football fits for Legette. Kansas City's receiver room still carries uncertainty entering the new season. Rashee Rice's future availability remains a question, while Xavier Worthy continues to develop into a complete receiver. Behind them, the Chiefs are relying on players such as Tyquan Thornton, Jalen Royals and Nikko Remigio to compete for meaningful snaps.Legette would give Patrick Mahomes another athletic outside target without requiring a major financial commitment. His combination of size and speed remains attractive despite an inconsistent start to his NFL career.Playing in Andy Reid's offense could also provide the fresh start many believe he needs after producing just 860 receiving yards across his first two seasons.The Baltimore Ravens also make plenty of sense. Zay Flowers remains Lamar Jackson's top receiving weapon, but Baltimore has continued searching for more production from the rest of its receiver group. Rashod Bateman's long-term future has generated speculation, while rookie Ja'Kobi Lane adds another young option to the rotation.Legette would not have to arrive as Baltimore's primary target. Instead, he could compete for snaps while giving Jackson another big-bodied receiver capable of creating yards after the catch. The Ravens have successfully developed several players whose value dipped elsewhere, making this another situation worth monitoring if Carolina decides to listen to offers.USA Today's Anthony Rizzuti recently explored four potential landing spots for Legette, identifying both the Kansas City Chiefs and the Baltimore Ravens as realistic destinations if the Carolina Panthers make the former first-round pick available.Rizzuti pointed to Kansas City's need for another dependable receiver behind Rashee Rice and Xavier Worthy, particularly after Rice's injury history and off-field issues created uncertainty around the position. He also noted that Baltimore could view Legette as a worthwhile addition if the club wants more competition behind Zay Flowers while continuing to reshape its receiving corps. The report comes after Legette's role became increasingly uncertain in Carolina following the arrivals of Tetairoa McMillan and Chris Brazzell II, along with Jalen Coker's continued emergence.Legette remains on the four-year, $12.36 million rookie contract he signed after being selected in the first round of the 2024 NFL Draft.According to Spotrac, he is scheduled to earn a base salary of $1.92 million during the 2026 season while carrying a cap hit of $3.37 million. His contract is fully guaranteed and runs through the 2028 campaign, giving any acquiring team multiple seasons of cost-controlled production before he becomes an unrestricted free agent in 2029.For Kansas City or Baltimore, that contract makes Legette a relatively low-risk investment. Neither team would be taking on a significant financial burden, while both would gain a young receiver with first-round pedigree. From Carolina's perspective, moving Legette could help recover draft capital while clearing a path for McMillan, Jalen Coker and Chris Brazzell II to take on larger offensive roles.For the Panthers, a trade would represent another step in reshaping the receiving room around Bryce Young. McMillan quickly established himself as the team's top target, while Coker has continued to earn a larger role. Brazzell's arrival has only increased competition, leaving Legette fighting for opportunities. Acquiring draft compensation now may carry more value than keeping him in a limited role.Kansas City could benefit immediately if Legette reaches the potential that made him a first-round selection. Mahomes has consistently elevated receivers in Reid's offense, and Legette's size would add something different alongside Worthy and Rice. His affordable contract also gives the Chiefs roster flexibility over the next three seasons.Baltimore offers another appealing opportunity. The Ravens would add a physical receiver without making a major financial commitment, while Legette would have the chance to develop alongside Lamar Jackson in one of the league's most efficient offenses. With training camp approaching, both the Kansas City Chiefs and Baltimore Ravens remain teams worth watching if the latest NFL trade rumors surrounding Xavier Legette continue to gain momentum.
Article Summary: Xavier legette's future with the carolina panthers has become one of the more intriguing storylines ahead of training camp. Usa today's anthony rizzuti named the kansas city chiefs and baltimore ravens among the most logical destinations if the panthers decide to move on from the 25-year-old wide receiver.

Article Title: Neymar's girlfriend Bruna Biancardi sends cryptic and powerful message after Brazil legend announces retirement
Article Text:
Bruna Biancardi reaffirmed that she is more than a "trophy wife" as Neymar's international career came to an emotional end following Brazil's World Cup exit to Norway. The influencer said she has her own career, team and financial independence while supporting Neymar through his football journey. After Brazil's defeat, Neymar announced his retirement from international football, ending his Brazil career with 80 goals, 59 assists and multiple major honours.

Neymar's girlfriend Bruna Biancardi says she is not a ' trophy wife '

Neymar retires from Brazil duty after World Cup heartbreak

Neymar’s emotional farewell to international football came just weeks after a deeply personal milestone in his family life. Brazil’s World Cup campaign may have ended in disappointment, but long-term girlfriend Bruna Biancardi has continued to make headlines for a different reason. The influencer and entrepreneur recently addressed the notion that she is just the football star’s “trophy wife”, explaining that she has developed an independent career while supporting Neymar through one of the biggest moments of his career.As Neymar represented Brazil at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Biancardi was also celebrating a major family milestone. The couple announced they are expecting another child together, revealing they are set to welcome a baby girl. It will be their third child as a couple, while Neymar will become a father of five.Biancardi shared the announcement on Instagram by teasing a YouTube video, writing:"We've got some news to tell you.. 🤍 Full Video on the YouTube Channel :)."Neymar reacted with excitement in the comments, posting:"I'm going crazy 😂 😂 😂 😂. 4 beautiful girls 😍."Away from their growing family, Biancardi has also spoken openly about her own professional journey.During an appearance on the Elas Que Jogam podcast, she rejected the idea that her success depends on Neymar."People think we don't work, that we stay at home, that we're trophy wives," Biancardi explained."I say, 'Wait a minute, because I have my jobs, I pay my team, I have salaries to pay. I earn my money, I pay my bills.'"The social media personality has built a significant online presence with nearly 16 million Instagram followers and continues working with brands while managing her own business commitments.Earlier in Brazil's World Cup campaign, she also posted an emotional tribute after Neymar secured his place in Carlo Ancelotti's squad."I also saw how Brazil ASKED FOR YOU!" she wrote before adding, "Me and our family will always be with you: in joy or sadness, in victory or defeat, in good days or in bad times..We love you infinitely. May God bless you, protect you and guard you every step of the way. Now is your time!"Brazil's World Cup campaign came to an end with a 2-1 Round of 16 defeat against Norway, a match that also marked the final international appearance of one of the country's greatest players.Neymar scored Brazil's only goal from the penalty spot deep into stoppage time, taking his international tally to 80 goals and 59 assists across 130 appearances.However, the strike was not enough to prevent elimination as Erling Haaland's brace sent Norway into the quarterfinals.Following the defeat, the 34-year-old confirmed that his international career had come to an end."I tried. I tried. It started here at Met Life stadium and I finished here. It is now over," Neymar told reporters after the match.The decision closes a remarkable chapter in Brazilian football.Neymar leaves as the nation's all-time leading scorer and helped deliver memorable successes, including the 2013 FIFA Confederations Cup and Olympic gold on home soil in 2016.Although injuries limited his role during parts of the World Cup, his experience remained valuable inside the squad.Now, with his Brazil career officially over, Neymar begins a new phase focused on club football and family life, while Biancardi continues to balance motherhood with the independent career she says she has worked hard to build.
Article Summary: Bruna biancardi says she has her own career, team and financial independence. The influencer said she is more than a "trophy wife" as neymar's international career came to an emotional end following brazil's world cup exit to norway. After brazil's defeat, neymar announced his retirement from international football.

Article Title: Erling Haaland mocks Brazil with laughing post after ending Selecao's World Cup dream
Article Text:
Erling Haaland inspired Norway to a stunning 2-1 victory over Brazil in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16, scoring twice to book his nation's first-ever quarterfinal appearance. After the match, the Manchester City striker posted "Well well well" on Instagram, delighting fans. Haaland now has seven tournament goals, matching Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappé in the Golden Boot race while setting multiple World Cup scoring records for Norway.

Erling Haaland reacts after firing Norway past Brazil

Erling Haaland continues record-breaking FIFA World Cup campaign

Erling Haaland needed just one social media post to capture the mood after producing one of the biggest performances of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The Norway striker scored twice in a dramatic 2-1 Round of 16 victory over Brazil before sharing a light-hearted reaction online that quickly grabbed fans' attention. His latest heroics not only ended Brazil's title hopes but also sent Norway into uncharted territory with a place in the quarterfinals.Haaland once again proved why he is one of world football's most feared forwards. Norway had to remain patient against a Brazil side that missed several chances, including a first-half penalty from Bruno Guimaraes that goalkeeper Orjan Nyland comfortably saved. Brazil also came close through substitute Endrick, who failed to convert a clear opportunity after a clever pass from Vinicius Junior.The breakthrough finally arrived in the 79th minute. Haaland rose above Brazil's defence to meet a cross before calmly beating Alisson with a composed finish. Just 11 minutes later, he struck again, driving a precise effort into the corner to put Norway in complete control.Brazil managed to pull one goal back, but the late response was not enough to stop Norway from securing a famous victory over the five-time world champions.Shortly after the final whistle, Haaland celebrated the result with a brief Instagram post that read:"Well well well😂."The simple message quickly spread across social media as fans celebrated Norway's biggest World Cup achievement in decades. The victory also booked Norway's first-ever appearance in a FIFA World Cup quarterfinal, where they will face the winner of the Mexico versus England Round of 16 clash in Miami on July 10.Haaland's tournament statistics continue to place him among the greatest World Cup starts ever seen. The Manchester City striker has now scored in each of his four appearances at the tournament and sits alongside Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe on seven goals in the race for the Golden Boot.His latest brace also added several remarkable milestones to an already memorable campaign. According to Opta, Haaland's seven goals in his first four World Cup matches are the most by any player since Gerd Muller scored eight during the 1970 tournament.He has also become only the fourth player to score four match-winning goals in a single World Cup. The exclusive list includes Gerd Muller, Salvatore Schillaci and Grzegorz Lato, placing the Norwegian among some of football's most iconic tournament performers.Haaland's journey has been impressive from the opening stages. He scored braces against Iraq and Senegal in the group stage before missing Norway's defeat to France. He returned for the knockout round against Ivory Coast and scored the winning goal before delivering another match-winning display against Brazil.Norway will now carry growing belief into the quarterfinals.With Haaland producing goals in crucial moments and the team showing resilience against elite opposition, the Scandinavian side has transformed from tournament outsiders into genuine contenders. Their dream run continues, and Haaland remains firmly at the heart of it.
Article Summary: Erling haaland scored twice as norway beat brazil 2-1 in the fifa world cup round of 16. The manchester city striker posted a light-hearted instagram message after the match. Norway will now face the winner of mexico vs england in the quarterfinals.

Article Title: "It’s fate ": Erling Haaland's girlfriend Isabel Haugseng Johansen shares heartfelt relationship admission during World Cup run
Article Text:
Erling Haaland's girlfriend, Isabel Haugseng Johansen, has offered a rare insight into their relationship during Norway's World Cup campaign. She admitted she initially liked Haaland's best friend before eventually falling for the Manchester City striker. Johansen has been supporting Norway throughout the tournament, embracing the team's cowboy-themed celebrations and famous Viking Row. The couple, who share a son, continue to enjoy family life while Haaland leads Norway's World Cup charge.

Isabel Haugseng Johansen recalls how her relationship with Erling Haaland began

Isabel Haugseng Johansen embraces Norway's World Cup journey



As Erling Haaland continues leading Norway's memorable FIFA World Cup campaign, his girlfriend, Isabel Haugseng Johansen, has also found herself in the spotlight. From supporting the team in the stands to sharing behind-the-scenes moments from the tournament, Johansen has been documenting the experience while also reflecting on the couple's relationship. Her earlier admission about how she and Haaland first met has resurfaced as fans continue following Norway's journey.Johansen previously revealed that Haaland was not the first person who caught her attention when they were younger. Speaking to The Sun in October 2025, she admitted that she initially liked one of his close friends before eventually developing feelings for the Manchester City striker."I only knew your best friend, so he was the one who I liked first, but then when I saw you I changed my view," Johansen said. "I think it’s fate but you don’t."Haaland remembers the story a little differently. He believes football played a major role in bringing them together because Johansen also played the sport growing up."She played football, we met and all of that, it’s nice because you understand football," Haaland explained. "She remembers the many times she saw me when we were younger, but I don’t. I had that YouTube video singing with my friends."The couple have remained together throughout Haaland's rise to becoming one of world football's biggest stars, with Johansen frequently supporting him during club and international matches.Johansen has been one of Norway's most enthusiastic supporters throughout the World Cup in the United States. Ahead of Norway's Round of 16 clash with Brazil, she shared a playful Instagram Story showing herself using a rowing machine at the gym."Better start practicing the (row) for tomorrow," she joked, referring to Norway supporters' popular Viking Row celebration.She has also embraced the tournament's American atmosphere, wearing a cowboy hat while cheering Norway during their victory over Ivory Coast in Dallas, matching Haaland's own fondness for the western-inspired look.Writing for Vogue Scandinavia, Johansen described how special the Viking Row celebration has become during the tournament."(Once) the Viking Row started, there was no way I was staying in my seat," she wrote. "From Oslo to Times Square, the row has been a special part of this tournament. I get goosebumps every single time we do this."... One last flight, straight to bed and then it’s time to do it all again. That’s the rhythm of a World Cup, and I wouldn’t have it any other way."Away from football, Haaland has often spoken about fatherhood after the couple welcomed their son. The Norway captain previously admitted becoming a parent has helped him mentally switch off from football."And to be honest I think with a kid, it makes me even better because I actually disconnect more than ever," Haaland said. "I don’t think of football at all... But when I go home, I relax even more so I think I need to give a shoutout to my son!"Johansen also revealed that the family even bought a pair of cowboy boots for their son during their World Cup adventure, adding another memorable chapter to Norway's tournament experience.
Article Summary: Isabel haugseng johansen has offered a rare insight into her relationship with erling haaland. She admitted she initially liked his best friend before falling for the manchester city striker. The couple, who share a son, continue to enjoy family life while he leads norway's world cup charge.

Article Title: Donald Trump intervention forces stunning FIFA U-turn as Folarin Balogun escapes World Cup ban
Article Text:
FIFA's recent decision to rescind Folarin Balogun's red card has sparked controversy, enabling the US talent to compete against Belgium. (AP Photo)

In an unprecedented development that has sent shockwaves across the footballing world, FIFA has officially overturned the red-card suspension of United States star striker Folarin Balogun. The extraordinary ruling clears the tournament’s standout forward to feature in Monday’s blockbuster Round of 16 clash against Belgium, following a direct intervention from US President Donald Trump Balogun, who has spearheaded the host nation's campaign with three goals, was slapped with an automatic one-match ban after receiving a highly controversial straight red card for an awkward ankle challenge on Bosnia and Herzegovina's Tarik Muharemović during their 2-0 Round of 32 victory. However, Sunday's bombshell announcement revealed that the ban has been deferred into a one-year probationary period. This marks the first time since 1962 that a red card shown during the World Cup proper has not resulted in an immediate suspension.The stunning reversal came to light after President Donald Trump placed a personal phone call to FIFA President Gianni Infantino to request a formal review of the incident.Following the announcement, Trump publicly expressed gratitude to football's governing body for reversing what he labelled "a great injustice," according to the Associated Press (AP).Conversely, the Belgian camp was left absolutely furious. Belgium manager Rudi Garcia fiercely mocked the governing body, stating he did not realize July 5th was April Fools' Day in Europe, while the Royal Belgian Football Association confirmed they are investigating all legal options to preserve the integrity of the sport.United States head coach Mauricio Pochettino aggressively defended the resolution, arguing that his team had already been unfairly punished by playing with ten men for over 30 minutes during the match.The availability of the 25-year-old Monaco frontman significantly boosts American hopes of reaching the quarter-finals for the first time since 2002. With history and political drama intertwining on the pitch, this high-stakes showdown against Belgium has transformed into the most anticipated and contentious battle of the tournament.
Article Summary: Fifa have overturned the red-card suspension of folarin balogun. Us president donald trump called fifa president gianni infantino to request a formal review of the incident. Balogun received a straight red card for an awkward ankle challenge on bosnia and herzegovina's tarik muharemović during their 2-0 round of 32 victory.
